user_query:
We’re standardizing real-time news alerts across multiple teams and need a clean, ready-to-register alert plan. Please do the following using the files under input/:

1) Read inputs:
- input/departments.csv — department_id and a semicolon-separated list of seed keywords per team
- input/notify_prefs.json — per-department notification preference (true/false)
- input/synonyms.json — keyword → list of synonyms that may be included if within limits
- input/stopwords.txt — whole-word stopwords to exclude from keywords
- input/constraints.yaml — constraints like max_keywords_per_alert and naming rules

2) Build one alert definition per department with this schema:
{
  "alert_name": "<lowercase_snake_case_department_id>_news_alert",
  "department_id": "<exact department_id from CSV>",
  "keywords": ["..."],
  "notify": true|false
}

Rules:
- Normalize all keywords: trim whitespace, lowercase, replace ‘&’ with ‘and’, collapse consecutive spaces to one, remove surrounding punctuation, and allow only letters, numbers, spaces, and hyphens.
- Remove any whole-word stopwords from keywords after normalization (case-insensitive).
- Expand synonyms from input/synonyms.json only if adding them does not exceed max_keywords_per_alert from constraints; prioritize keeping the base keyword first, then add synonyms in alphabetical order until the limit is reached. If expansion would exceed the limit, include only the base keyword and the alphabetically earliest synonyms that fit.
- De-duplicate keywords within each department after normalization and synonym expansion.
- Ensure 1 ≤ keywords.length ≤ max_keywords_per_alert.
- Sort the final keywords array alphabetically (ascending) for each alert.
- Set notify per department from input/notify_prefs.json (default to false if missing).
- alert_name must be lowercase snake_case department_id + “_news_alert”.

3) Write outputs:
- output/alerts.jsonl — one JSON object per line, one per department, following the exact schema and rules above.
- output/report.csv — with header: department_id,alert_name,notify,num_keywords,keywords_joined. Include one row per department where keywords_joined is a single semicolon-separated, alphabetically ordered string of the final keywords.
- output/global_keywords.json — a JSON object mapping each unique keyword to an array of department_ids that use it (sorted alphabetically). This must be exactly consistent with output/alerts.jsonl.
- output/README.md — a short rationale with the sections “Approach”, “Assumptions”, and “Next Steps” explaining how you normalized terms, handled stopwords and synonyms, and ensured limit compliance. Keep it professional and concise (at least 3 paragraphs).

Do not call any external services; just prepare the registration-ready files above based on the inputs.
